channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Kalshi users are in an uproar over their Time ‘Person of the Year’ bets
item.description: Time magazine has named the “Architects of AI” as its 2025 Person of the Year, a decision that has sparked significant backlash from gamblers who lost out on semantics.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Before megalodon, researchers say a monstrous shark ruled ancient Australian seas
item.description: Researchers have dated vertebrae from a massive prehistoric shark thought to have ruled the waves off northern Australia back to further in the Cretaceous period than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Trump is blocking U.S. states from regulating artificial intelligence
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday aimed at blocking states from crafting their own regulations for artificial intelligence, saying the burgeoning industry is at risk of being stifled by a patchwork of onerous rules while in a battle with Chinese competitors for supremacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Arc Raiders Cold Snap update release date
item.description: The Arc Raiders Cold Snap update arrives soon. Here's the release date, and what we know about new quests, map conditions, and more.
item.dc:creator: Jake Green, TechRadar

item.title: The mystery behind the hellish Game Awards statue has been solved as Larian Studios unveils its next game, Divinity
item.description: Baldur's Gate 3 developer Larian Studios has announced its next major game, Divinity, at The Game Awards 2025.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Trouble in Toyland: New A.I. pals can discuss sex, God, playing with matches
item.description: Granted, it's unlikely a 5-year-old would ask an A.I. buddy about kink. But researchers say parents should know that such toys are programmed to give a lot of detailed, sometimes graphic answers.
item.dc:creator: Teri Sforza, The Mercury News

item.title: Don’t blame AI if the data doesn’t stack up
item.description: Agentic systems are increasingly operating in agent-to-human and agent-to-agent scenarios, driving decisions and automating operations across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: After Nvidia’s White House ‘coup,’ China may not be buying
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company,covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Trump signs executive order to block state AI regulations
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to block states from regulating artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI and Microsoft are facing a lawsuit over ChatGPT’s alleged role in this murder-suicide
item.description: The heirs of an 83-year-old Connecticut woman are suing ChatGPT maker OpenAI and its business partner Microsoft for wrongful death, alleging that the artificial intelligence chatbot intensified her son’s “paranoid delusions” and helped direct them at his mother before he killed her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AI executive order could deepen trust crisis, not solve it
item.description: If the regulatory ecosystem was already messy and difficult to navigate, then a nationwide legal battle over AI and federalism will only intensify the problem. The post AI executive order could deepen trust crisis, not solve it first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI is stepping into the fight for supply chain resilience and battlefield readiness
item.description: "I do think there's going to be a bit of a learning curve tempered by the fact that a lot of people are using large language models now," said John Garrity. The post AI is stepping into the fight for supply chain resilience and battlefield readiness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Adapt or be deceived: The shape-shifting nature of fraud
item.description: As digital innovation evolves, so too does the surface area for fraud. And with each advance, deception quickly fills loopholes designers never intended to leave. Yet, as we close 2025, what’s changing now isn’t the intent, it’s the instrumentation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Nvidia software gives data centers deeper visibility into GPU thermals and reliability
item.description: Nvidia has released new open-source software that gives data center operators deeper visibility into the thermal and overall health of its AI GPUs, aiming to help enterprises manage heat and reliability challenges as power-hungry accelerators push cooling systems to their limits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US state attorneys general ask AI giants to fix ‘delusional’ outputs
item.description: After disturbing mental health incidents involving AI chatbots, state attorneys general sent a letter to major AI companies, warning them to fix “delusional outputs” or risk legal action, TechCrunch reported Wednesday. The letter, signed by 42 attorneys general from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Study finds humans were making fire 400,000 years ago, far earlier than once thought
item.description: Archaeologists in Britain say they have found the earliest known evidence of deliberate fire-making, dating to around 400,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Mustakim Hasnath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GitHub Action Secrets aren’t secret anymore: exposed PATs now a direct path into cloud environments
item.description: Many enterprises use GitHub Action Secrets to store and protect sensitive information such as credentials, API keys, and tokens used in CI/CD workflows. These private repositories are widely assumed to be safe and locked down.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI agents: The next layer of federal digital infrastructure
item.description: AI agents can help agencies improve citizen services, accelerate national security decision-making, and scale mission delivery in ways once unthinkable. The post AI agents: The next layer of federal digital infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The hidden cost of Amazon Nova 2
item.description: Amazon’s Nova 2 announcement at AWS re:Invent 2025 is exactly the type of AI offering we expected from AWS and, frankly, exactly what should make thoughtful architects nervous. Nova 2 is positioned as a frontier-grade model, tightly integrated with Amazon Bedrock.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI-assisted software development with Amazon Q Developer
item.description: When I reviewed Amazon Q Developer in 2024, I noted that it was able to generate whole functions in common programming languages with only a few fixes. It was useful for completing lines of code, doc strings, and if/for/while/try code blocks as you type.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why parents may want to delay smartphones for kids
item.description: New study of 10,500+ kids reveals early smartphone ownership linked to depression, obesity, and poor sleep by age 12. Earlier phones mean higher risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
